title: Velocity PID for Intake and Shooter Wheels
subtitle: Velocity control is about holding speed under load, not merely sending a motor percentage.
summary: The 2024 robot used velocity PID loops for intake and shooter mechanisms.

artifact.title: Speed response to a setpoint
artifact.description: Change gain and damping to see why a wheel can overshoot, undershoot, or settle.

sections.heading: Why Percent Output Is Not Enough
body: A shooter wheel slows when it contacts a game piece. A fixed motor percentage cannot know that happened.
Velocity feedback measures actual wheel speed and pushes the motor harder when the measured speed falls below target.

sections.heading: Feedforward plus Feedback
body: Many mechanisms use feedforward for the expected voltage and PID feedback for correction. The feedforward gets close; the feedback cleans up error.

pitfalls: Tuning shooter speed with no game piece interaction.
Ignoring sensor units and loop period.
Using position PID constants for velocity control.

quiz.question: What is the measured variable in velocity PID?
quiz.explanation: Velocity PID compares target speed with measured speed.
